figures.caption: Table 14: GEE coefficients for Atomic KBQA experiments
Table 3: Summary of GEE coefficients (∗ ∗ 𝑝 < 0.01, ∗𝑝 < 0.05). Atomic KBQA results include GrailQA, WebQSP, and GraphQ.

Table 2: Comparison of FH and SH across structured (KBQA) and unstructured (multi-objective HotpotQA) tool-calling tasks. FH matches SH in accuracy across most settings while using substantially fewer input tokens than SH. The efficiency gap widens as the tool set grows (e.g. KQA Pro).

Table 17: FH with Gemini-3-Flash under low-robustness retrieval (top-1) on multi-objective HotpotQA (𝑘 = 2). This table focuses on one sub-question: “Which director, John Schlesinger or Barbara Albert, was also a writer and film producer?”
Table 8: Atomic query tools defined by Luo et al. [23] with modifications, and their correspondence to functions in Sexpression [10].
Table 12: GEE coefficients for KQA Pro experiments (1/2)

Table 16: SH with Gemini-3-Flash under low-robustness retrieval (top-1) on multi-objective HotpotQA (𝑘 = 2). This table focuses on one sub-question: “Which director, John Schlesinger or Barbara Albert, was also a writer and film producer?”
Table 7: KoPL tools defined by Cao et al. [3].
Table 4: Performance change (absolute accuracy difference) under low robustness settings. Difference larger than 0.05 is denoted in bold.

Table 5: Fraction of task instances in which the agent made at least one repeated tool call (same tool call with identical arguments). SH exhibits substantially higher repetition rates than FH in several settings. Rates ≥ 10% are shown in bold.

Figure 3: Distribution of task instances across datasets. The horizontal and vertical axes represent the critical path length (depth) and average parallelism (breadth), respectively. The bar chart summarizes total instance counts per dataset.
Table 13: GEE coefficients for KQA Pro experiments (2/2)
Table 11: A list of software used in this study.
Table 15: GEE coefficients for multi-objective HotpotQA experiments

Table 6: Gemini-3-Flash under low-robustness retrieval (top1) on multi-objective HotpotQA (𝑘 = 2). This table focuses on one sub-question: “Which director, John Schlesinger or Barbara Albert, was also a writer and film producer?” Italic text shows the model’s internal reasoning. “Error” denotes execution failures. The feedback text returned to the agent is omitted for brevity. See Appendix C.2 for full trajectories.
Table 1: Example task instances with their corresponding ground-truth tool-call trajectories (execution graphs).
